Name: UMaterial
Description:
A Material is an asset which can be applied to a mesh to control the visual look of the scene.
  When light from the scene hits the surface, the shading model of the material is used to calculate how that light interacts with the surface.
 
  Warning: Creating new materials directly increases shader compile times!  Consider creating a Material Instance off of an existing material instead.

Variables.Name: PhysMaterial
Variables.Description: Physical material to use for this graphics material. Used for sounds, effects etc.

Variables.Name: MaterialDomain
Variables.Description:
The domain that the material's attributes will be evaluated in.
	  Certain pieces of material functionality are only valid in certain domains, for example vertex normal is only valid on a surface.

Variables.Name: BlendMode
Variables.Description: Determines how the material's color is blended with background colors.

Variables.Name: DecalBlendMode
Variables.Description: Defines how the GBuffer chanels are getting manipulated by a decal material pass. (only with MaterialDomain == MD_DeferredDecal)

Variables.Name: MaterialDecalResponse
Variables.Description:
Defines how the material reacts on DBuffer decals (Affects look, performance and texturesample usage).
	  Non DBuffer Decals can be disabled on the primitive (e.g. static mesh)

Variables.Name: ShadingModel
Variables.Description: Determines how inputs are combined to create the material's final color.

Variables.Name: OpacityMaskClipValue
Variables.Description: If BlendMode is BLEND_Masked, the surface is not rendered where OpacityMask < OpacityMaskClipValue.

Variables.Name: VertexOffsetAlongNormal
Variables.Description: pixels offset along vertex normal, for outline drawing.

Variables.Name: bCastDynamicShadowAsMasked
Variables.Description:
If true, translucent materials will cast dynamic shadows according to their opacity.
	 OpacityMaskClipValue is used as the threshold value.

Variables.Name: WorldPositionOffset
Variables.Description: Adds to world position in the vertex shader.

Variables.Name: WorldDisplacement
Variables.Description: Offset in world space applied to tessellated vertices.

Variables.Name: TessellationMultiplier
Variables.Description: Multiplies the tessellation factors applied when a tessellation mode is set.

Variables.Name: SubsurfaceColor
Variables.Description: Inner material color, only used for ShadingModel=Subsurface

Variables.Name: AmbientOcclusion
Variables.Description: output ambient occlusion to the GBuffer

Variables.Name: Refraction
Variables.Description:
output refraction index for translucent rendering
	  Air:1.0 Water:1.333 Ice:1.3 Glass:~1.6 Diamond:2.42

Variables.Name: CustomizedUVs
Variables.Description:
These inputs are evaluated in the vertex shader and allow artists to do arbitrary vertex shader operations and access them in the pixel shader.
	  When unconnected or hidden they default to passing through the vertex UVs.

Variables.Name: bEnableSeparateTranslucency
Variables.Description: Indicates that the material should be rendered in the SeparateTranslucency Pass (not affected by DOF, requires bAllowSeparateTranslucency to be set in .ini).

Variables.Name: bTranslucencyRenderAfterSS
Variables.Description: Indicates that the material should be rendered after post process and super sampling, dedicate for reticle materials

Variables.Name: bEnableMobileSeparateTranslucency
Variables.Description: Indicates that the translucent material should not be affected by bloom or DOF. (Note: Depth testing is not available)

Variables.Name: bEnableMobileDownsampleSeparateTranslucency
Variables.Description: Indicates that the translucent material can be rendered on an off-screen render target at a low resolution)

Variables.Name: bEnableResponsiveAA
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material should be rendered using responsive anti-aliasing. Improves sharpness of small moving particles such as sparks.
	  Only use for small moving features because it will cause aliasing of the background.

Variables.Name: bScreenSpaceReflections
Variables.Description: SSR on translucency

Variables.Name: TwoSided
Variables.Description: Indicates that the material should be rendered without backface culling and the normal should be flipped for backfaces.

Variables.Name: DitheredLODTransition
Variables.Description: Whether meshes rendered with the material should support dithered LOD transitions.

Variables.Name: DitherOpacityMask
Variables.Description: Dither opacity mask. When combined with Temporal AA this can be used as a form of limited translucency which supports all lighting features.

Variables.Name: bAllowNegativeEmissiveColor
Variables.Description: Whether the material should allow outputting negative emissive color values.  Only allowed on unlit materials.

Variables.Name: NumCustomizedUVs
Variables.Description: Number of customized UV inputs to display.  Unconnected customized UV inputs will just pass through the vertex UVs.

Variables.Name: TranslucencyLightingMode
Variables.Description: Sets the lighting mode that will be used on this material if it is translucent.

Variables.Name: TranslucencyDirectionalLightingIntensity
Variables.Description:
Useful for artificially increasing the influence of the normal on the lighting result for translucency.
	  A value larger than 1 increases the influence of the normal, a value smaller than 1 makes the lighting more ambient.

Variables.Name: AllowTranslucentCustomDepthWrites
Variables.Description: Allows a translucenct material to be used with custom depth writing by compiling additional shaders.

Variables.Name: TranslucentShadowDensityScale
Variables.Description: Scale used to make translucent shadows more or less opaque than the material's actual opacity.

Variables.Name: TranslucentSelfShadowDensityScale
Variables.Description:
Scale used to make translucent self-shadowing more or less opaque than the material's shadow on other objects.
	  This is only used when the object is casting a volumetric translucent shadow.

Variables.Name: TranslucentSelfShadowSecondDensityScale
Variables.Description: Used to make a second self shadow gradient, to add interesting shading in the shadow of the first.

Variables.Name: TranslucentSelfShadowSecondOpacity
Variables.Description: Controls the strength of the second self shadow gradient.

Variables.Name: TranslucentBackscatteringExponent
Variables.Description:
Controls how diffuse the material's backscattering is when using the MSM_Subsurface shading model.
	  Larger exponents give a less diffuse look (smaller, brighter backscattering highlight).
	  This is only used when the object is casting a volumetric translucent shadow from a directional light.

Variables.Name: TranslucentMultipleScatteringExtinction
Variables.Description:
Colored extinction factor used to approximate multiple scattering in dense volumes.
	  This is only used when the object is casting a volumetric translucent shadow.

Variables.Name: TranslucentShadowStartOffset
Variables.Description: Local space distance to bias the translucent shadow.  Positive values move the shadow away from the light.

Variables.Name: bDisableDepthTest
Variables.Description: Whether to draw on top of opaque pixels even if behind them. This only has meaning for translucency.

Variables.Name: bGenerateSphericalParticleNormals
Variables.Description: Whether to generate spherical normals for particles that use this material.

Variables.Name: bTangentSpaceNormal
Variables.Description:
Whether the material takes a tangent space normal or a world space normal as input.
	  (TangentSpace requires extra instructions but is often more convenient).

Variables.Name: bUseEmissiveForDynamicAreaLighting
Variables.Description: If enabled, the material's emissive colour is injected into the LightPropagationVolume

Variables.Name: bBlockGI
Variables.Description: If enabled, the material's opacity defines how much GI is blocked when using the LightPropagationVolume feature

Variables.Name: bUseSimpleGI
Variables.Description: If enabled, the material uses simplified and inaccurate GI color for efficiency

Variables.Name: bUsedAsSpecialEngineMaterial
Variables.Description:
This is a special usage flag that allows a material to be assignable to any primitive type.
	  This is useful for materials used by code to implement certain viewmodes, for example the default material or lighting only material.
	  The cost is that nearly 20x more shaders will be compiled for the material than the average material, which will greatly increase shader compile time and memory usage.
	  This flag should only be enabled when absolutely necessary, and is purposefully not exposed to the UI to prevent abuse.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithSkeletalMesh
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with skeletal meshes.
	  This will result in the shaders required to support skeletal meshes being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithGFur
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with GFur.
	 This will result in the shaders required to support skeletal meshes being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithEditorCompositing
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with editor compositing
	  This will result in the shaders required to support editor compositing being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithParticleSprites
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with particle sprites
	  This will result in the shaders required to support particle sprites being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithBeamTrails
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with beam trails
	  This will result in the shaders required to support beam trails being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithMeshParticles
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with mesh particles
	  This will result in the shaders required to support mesh particles being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithNiagaraSprites
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with Niagara sprites (meshes and ribbons, respectively)
	 This will result in the shaders required to support Niagara sprites being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithIBL
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with reflection cube
	  This will result in the shaders required to support IBL being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithStaticLighting
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with static lighting
	  This will result in the shaders required to support static lighting being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithMorphTargets
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with morph targets
	  This will result in the shaders required to support morph targets being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithSplineMeshes
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with spline meshes
	  This will result in the shaders required to support spline meshes being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithInstancedStaticMeshes
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with instanced static meshes
	  This will result in the shaders required to support instanced static meshes being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithCustomInstancedStaticMeshes
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with custom instanced static meshes
	  This will result in the shaders required to support instanced static meshes being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.

Variables.Name: bUsesDistortion
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with distortion
	  This will result in the shaders required to support distortion being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithClothing
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with clothing
	  This will result in the shaders required to support clothing being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithUI_DEPRECATED
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with Slate UI and UMG
	  This will result in the shaders required to support UI materials being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithPPRBackgroud
Variables.Description: Indicates that the material would use for ppr background in deferred rendering.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithSurfelInjectColor
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with SurfelGI inject color
	  This will result in the shaders required to support SurfelGI inject color being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.
	  HACK by huiwenjiang.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithTranslucentGI
Variables.Description:
[SurfelGI - brainfkli ADD]
	  Indicates that the material and its instances can be affected by GI in translucent blend mode.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithAtmosphericSkyBox
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with AtmosphericSkyBox
	  This will result in the shaders required to support AtmosphericSkyBox being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.

Variables.Name: bAutomaticallySetUsageInEditor
Variables.Description:
Whether to automatically set usage flags based on what the material is applied to in the editor.
	  It can be useful to disable this on a base material with many instances, where adding another usage flag accidentally (eg bUsedWithSkeletalMeshes) can add a lot of shader permutations.

Variables.Name: bFullyRough
Variables.Description: Forces the material to be completely rough. Saves a number of instructions and one sampler. Note: Overrided by Lite Rough.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithLandscapeDeform
Variables.Description:
Indicates that the material and its instances can be use with Landscape Deform
	  This will result in the shaders required to support LandscapeDeform being compiled which will increase shader compile time and memory usage.

Variables.Name: bUseFullPrecision
Variables.Description:
Forces this material to use full (highp) precision in the pixel shader.
	 	This is slower than the default (mediump) but can be used to work around precision-related rendering errors.
	 	This setting has no effect on older mobile devices that do not support high precision.
	   Note: Overrided by Lite Rough.

Variables.Name: bForceMaterialFloat
Variables.Description:
Forces this material's temporary variables to use full precision float in the pixel shader.
	  Keeps uniforms to use default precision. HACK by huiwen.

Variables.Name: bUseLightmapDirectionality
Variables.Description: Use lightmap directionality and per pixel normals. If disabled, lighting from lightmaps will be flat but cheaper.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithDynamicInstancing
Variables.Description: Indicates that the each material instance(of this material) can be dynamic instanced.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithPhotonShadow
Variables.Description: #if WITH_PHOTON_SHADOW

Variables.Name: bUsedWithDynamicBatching
Variables.Description: Indicates that the material instance shared with same base mat can be batched

Variables.Name: bUseLegacySpecular
Variables.Description: use Phong instead of GGX

Variables.Name: bLiteRough
Variables.Description: Override: Fully Rough On、UseFullPrecision Off

Variables.Name: bInstL2WOnlyTranslation
Variables.Description: Instancing only uses translation of LocalToWorld, exclusive of rotation and scale.

Variables.Name: bUseHQForwardReflections
Variables.Description:
Forward renderer: enables multiple parallax-corrected reflection captures that blend together.
	  Mobile renderer: blend between nearest 3 reflection captures, but reduces the number of samplers available to the material as two more samplers will be used for reflection cubemaps.

Variables.Name: bUsePlanarForwardReflections
Variables.Description: Enables planar reflection when using the forward renderer or mobile. Enabling this setting reduces the number of samplers available to the material as one more sampler will be used for the planar reflection.

Variables.Name: bApplyVertexFog
Variables.Description: When false, materials are not fogged in forward shading or mobile. Defaults to true.

Variables.Name: bNormalCurvatureToRoughness
Variables.Description: Reduce roughness based on screen space normal changes.

Variables.Name: D3D11TessellationMode
Variables.Description: The type of tessellation to apply to this object.  Note D3D11 required for anything except MTM_NoTessellation.

Variables.Name: bEnableCrackFreeDisplacement
Variables.Description: Prevents cracks in the surface of the mesh when using tessellation.

Variables.Name: bEnableAdaptiveTessellation
Variables.Description: Enables adaptive tessellation, which tries to maintain a uniform number of pixels per triangle.

Variables.Name: bUsedWithTexture2DArrayShaderVariant
Variables.Description:
ENABLE_TEXTURE2D_ARRAY_SHADER_VARIANT
	 Enable Dynamic MaterialInstance use Texture 2D Array shader variant with Texture 2D material expression graph

Variables.Name: bSkipRSH
Variables.Description: Skip Runtime Static Batching (RSH)

Variables.Name: Wireframe
Variables.Description: Enables a wireframe view of the mesh the material is applied to.

Variables.Name: bOutputVelocityOnBasePass
Variables.Description: Skips outputting velocity during the base pass.

Variables.Name: bUnlitOutputAllMTOnBasePass
Variables.Description: Force unlit material output all MT during the base pass.

Variables.Name: ShadingRate
Variables.Description: Select what shading rate to apply for platforms that have variable rate shading

Variables.Name: Expressions
Variables.Description: Array of material expressions, excluding Comments.  Used by the material editor.

Variables.Name: MaterialFunctionInfos
Variables.Description: Array of all functions this material depends on.

Variables.Name: MaterialParameterCollectionInfos
Variables.Description: Array of all parameter collections this material depends on.

Variables.Name: bCanMaskedBeAssumedOpaque
Variables.Description: true if this Material can be assumed Opaque when set to masked.

Variables.Name: bIsMasked_DEPRECATED
Variables.Description: true if Material is masked and uses custom opacity

Variables.Name: bIsPreviewMaterial
Variables.Description: true if Material is the preview material used in the material editor.

Variables.Name: bUseMaterialAttributes
Variables.Description: when true, the material attributes pin is used instead of the regular pins.

Variables.Name: bComputeFogPerPixel
Variables.Description: When true, translucent materials have fog computed for every pixel, which costs more but fixes artifacts due to low tessellation.

Variables.Name: bDisableDirectionalLighting
Variables.Description: When true, the directional lighting will be disabled

Variables.Name: bAllowDevelopmentShaderCompile
Variables.Description: If true the compilation environment will be changed to remove the global COMPILE_SHADERS_FOR_DEVELOPMENT flag.

Variables.Name: bIsMaterialEditorStatsMaterial
Variables.Description: true if this is a special material used for stats by the material editor.

Variables.Name: UsageFlagWarnings
Variables.Description: true if we have printed a warning about material usage for a given usage flag.

Variables.Name: BlendableLocation
Variables.Description: Where the node is inserted in the (post processing) graph, only used if domain is PostProcess

Variables.Name: BlendablePriority
Variables.Description: If multiple nodes with the same  type are inserted at the same point, this defined order and if they get combined, only used if domain is PostProcess

Variables.Name: BlendableOutputAlpha
Variables.Description: If this is enabled, the blendable will output alpha

Variables.Name: RefractionMode
Variables.Description: Controls how the Refraction input is interpreted and how the refraction offset into scene color is computed for this material.

Variables.Name: RefractionDepthBias
Variables.Description: This is the refraction depth bias, larger values offset distortion to prevent closer objects from rendering into the distorted surface at acute viewing angles but increases the disconnect between surface and where the refraction starts.

Variables.Name: bSimplePointLight
Variables.Description: Enable this so the material will not calculate spot light shadows

Variables.Name: StateId
Variables.Description:
Guid that uniquely identifies this material.
	  Any changes to the state of the material that do not appear separately in the shadermap DDC keys must cause this guid to be regenerated!
	  For example, a modification to the Expressions array.
	  Code changes that cause the guid to be regenerated on load should be avoided, as that requires a resave of the content to stop recompiling every load.

Variables.Name: ExpressionTextureReferences
Variables.Description:
Cached texture references from all expressions in the material (including nested functions).
	  This is used to link uniform texture expressions which were stored in the DDC with the UTextures that they reference.

Variables.Name: EditorComments
Variables.Description: Array of comments associated with this material; viewed in the material editor.

Variables.Name: ParameterGroupData
Variables.Description: Controls where this parameter group is displayed in a material instance parameter list.  The lower the number the higher up in the parameter list.
